If you ever need help, feel free to hit me up.
like, this line killed me for like ten minutes
> 不必挣扎。不必抗争。不必尝试理解不可理解之物。\n在无上意志的目光中，迎接梦境的终结。
it's very easy to translate literally but there's a lot of nuance in the way it's written that's hard to convey
That's a whole lotta language I can't speak
What is the literal translation?
the text literally says "No need to struggle. No need to resist. No need to try and understand the incomprehensible. In the gaze of the Supreme Will, welcome the end of the dream"
but like, the tone of it kind of conveys this devout worshipper's assurance that all struggle and resistance is meaningless
Honestly, that's not too bad. It could be touched up, and I could easily edit that
and thus why would you bother
the original english was "Do not struggle. Do not resist."
which is like
But I'd go for a softer, more alluring tone with it.
technically not *wrong* but it adds a level of imperative tone to it that doesn't really fit because it's not someone commanding you to not resist
it's someone going "why resist the inevitable?"
"No need to struggle. No need to resist. No need to try and understand something you cannot. In the gaze of the Supreme Will, submit to the end of the dream."
also the last part even though it's literally welcome is more of like, let us welcome/embrace
Something like that.

so what I ended up with was 
> There is no need to struggle. There is no need to resist. Why attempt to comprehend the incomprehensible?\nUnder the gaze of the Supreme Will, let us embrace the end of the dream.
second "There is" might be uneeded
but man that added a lot of words and also that took me like 10 minutes so doing this for the....literal tens of thousands of lines of text.....
Yeah, if you want, I can do most of the actual editing for you if you just tell me the type of tone you want me to convert it to.
from a writing standpoint yes but a lot of the word structures in these lines are purposefully repeated so I have to match the equivalent in english
Ahhh
Do you wanna match the structure or the tone ?
it's like a repeated word structure that's also unnecessary in the original chinese but is done deliberately to create a sort of emphasized voice
That makes sense to a degree. When I imagine someone with this, I imagine a softer tone, sort of like an actual embrace
A, "You don't need to do this. Submit to this."
yeah it's essentially a cultist of the end telling you to welcome their god so the tone should match

"extracting the silver core"

but it can also mean extract and purify

content: i used extract since that's what i'm used to when it comes to silver cores but I guess if the question is wording choice then refining is probably a better choice
distillation...might be possible? though that'd be a pretty big change with how much text has to be edited
"process", maybe
but honestly i'm kind of fuzzy on understanding what exactly they *do* to the silver cores to get awakeners out of them so I can't really comprehend the best verb for it
Yeah I think it's meant to be nebulous.

also here's another example of it:
> 那是意图斩破海洋之人的巨刃，是意图踏平诸海之人的野心。
currently translated as
> It was the colossal blade of one who intended to cleave the ocean, embodying the ambition to subjugate all seas.
which is technically valid but like, the original chinese has a very deliberate repeated structure of 是意图, 是意图 ("it is the intention of") in both the first half and second half so it was kind of a shame to lose that

so I changed it to
> It was the colossal blade of one who intended to cleave the ocean; it was the ambition of one who intended to subjugate all seas.
to kind of carry over that repeated structure
obviously quite unnecessary from a grammatical and writing standpoint but the repetition is the point there, at least imo
but yeah sorry enough nerd monologuing from me, I gotta finish up the story chapters

oh bleh this one is really hard and I don't know what to do

original text: 
> 对蚍蜉而言，生存与毁灭从不是问题。\n它们生来便是要面对巨树的。
current english text:
> To ants, survival and destruction are never an issue.\nThey are born to face the giant tree

issue: this line relies entirely on the chinese idiom 蚍蜉撼樹 which means "an ant trying to shake a tree" - it refers to someone trying to accomplish something far greater than they're capable of, i.e., someone who is massively overrating themselves

so a direct english translation makes no sense because the nuance is entirely lost, whereas the chinese conveys the idea that there is meaning in struggling against something much greater than they are, despite the futility
any ideas for something equivalent in english?
closest idiom I can think of is david vs goliath, but that doesn't work because david is supposed to win so it fails to demonstrate futility
That's a tough one for sure.

"Have" is used with "I," "you," "we," and "they," as well as with plural nouns. "Has" is used with "he," "she," and "it," as well as with singular nouns.

I went through all the source sheet - story and made some separated sheets, where I tried to leave as neat as possible for easier localization, if someone wants it as a base to use, or just see through the Key where it is localized in the original one. 
[Just take notion that Character (EN) and Proofread column, or the extra last column to input your own language do not exist in the source sheet, I made that for personal organization]
The image is one of them
Finally done with what I was busy with and have a few hours to sit down with this project. I'd like to revisit the STR discussion, but I'm gonna write up a thesis on it and put it up as a thread.

Yay! After two full hours of writing, I've finally put all of my thoughts on the matter onto paper, and have even wrote some examples of how I would implement the templating.
Unfortunately, now that I am exhausted, I see the error of my ways, and disagree with a primary point of my own argument.
congratulations for winning the battle against yourself
The STR Bonus Discussion
So, I wonder if we can request that the font size dynamically scale with message length.

Because even just in the Prologue, I'm running into more and more instances of text that forces that scroll effect, and while sometimes cutting the line break would fix that, more often than not it would still trigger the effect, no matter how succinct the sentences are.
And worst of all, you can’t click and drag to scroll up on the dialogue box... and in combat the history button isn't even there, so if you didn't read it fast enough, it's gone forever.
我知道，当年你被唤醒时，弥萨格曾许诺你，会与你一起战斗……战斗至这个星球的末日。
Does this mean "fight alongside you" or "fight you"? It's a little ambiguous, and I could see it going either way with how Lotan is written.
I believe that's a bug, since it only started happening with a recent patch
it should be fixed soon (hopefully)
it's explicitly "fight alongside you"
会与你一起战斗
"你一起" means "together with you"
